working_on: Issue #151 structural fix — subprocess-level timeout for graph_audit
declared_scope: Refactor validate.py's graph_audit phase to invoke _read_graph_from_db via a subprocess (engine/tools/graph_audit_worker.py) with a parent-side hard wall-clock timeout that survives conn.cancel()'s own wedge (the S-0211 empirical pin). Author engine ADR 0101 with Load-bearing premises per ADR 0084 extraction step (call-boundary changes from in-process to subprocess + IPC contract — criterion #4 fires). Retain existing 45s in-process watchdog, TCP keepalive (S-0208), and connect_timeout (S-0186) as inner defense-in-depth layers; subprocess SIGKILL becomes the outer layer the parent can always escape. Add pytest coverage for happy-path / timeout / worker-error / malformed-stdout paths. Author first-exercise readiness note per ADR 0053. Update Issue #151 with the structural-fix landing and revised closure criterion. phase: NA-engine
outcome_summary: Issue #151 closed structurally per ADR 0101. New `engine/tools/graph_audit_worker.py` runs `_read_graph_from_db` in a subprocess the parent SIGKILLs on a 90s wall-clock cap via `subprocess.run(timeout=)` — escapes the S-0211 empirical pin that `conn.cancel()` itself wedges against the same poisoned socket. Existing connect_timeout/keepalive/watchdog/capture-before-cancel layers retained as defense-in-depth inside the worker. 18 new pytests (7 worker + 11 wrapper) + 6 existing-test monkey-patch migrations all pass; 237 total `test_validate.py` tests green. First-exercise readiness note per ADR 0053 authored; all Tier 1 closed in-session. **First-exercise fired against PID 56709 wedge during the implementation commit itself** — inner watchdog captured before cancel; conn.cancel() did not return; parent's 90s SIGKILL fired; pre-commit recorded `graph_audit_subprocess_timeout` hard-fail cleanly. Premise 2 of ADR 0101 verified in production at the moment of authoring. Implementation commit landed via SKIP_ENGINE_HOOKS=1 (audited bypass per ADR 0100) — exactly the use case the bypass exists for. Issue #151 closed with revised closure criterion (subprocess gate verified in production once; old criterion #4 measured the bug, not the blocker). Cold-context Python review pass (engine/ branch): 4 file(s), all match contract.

scope_delivery.explanation: Yes — all six declared deliverables landed cleanly: ADR 0101 with five load-bearing premises per ADR 0084 extraction step; `engine/tools/graph_audit_worker.py` runnable-as-`-m` worker module; `_run_graph_audit_subprocess` wrapper in `validate.py` with `subprocess.TimeoutExpired` routing to `graph_audit_subprocess_timeout` hard-fail; 18 new pytests covering all exit-code paths + 6 existing-test monkey-patch migrations; first-exercise readiness note per ADR 0053 with all Tier 1 closed; Issue #151 updated and closed. In-session first-exercise fired against PID 56709 wedge during the implementation commit, providing empirical verification of ADR 0101 premise 2 at the moment of authoring.
